payload.answer: Store each (id, name, value) triplet as a separate memory card with explicit relationships for updates/deletions. Use metadata tags to resolve homonyms and mark superseded facts with `obsoleted_by` predicates. Never present deleted/superseded facts as current.
citations: Durability constraints for dynamic knowledge graphs (2022)

entity_resolution.reason: Preserves disambiguation without suppressing ambiguity in queries.

memory_cards.statement: A CSV table with 500 rows containing repeated (id, name, value) triplets should be decomposed into atomic memory cards to preserve granularity and avoid redundancy.
Each (id, name, value) triplet must be stored as a separate memory card with explicit relationships to avoid ambiguity in updates/deletions.
Ambiguity in (name, value) pairs (e.g., homonyms) must be resolved via explicit entity relationships or metadata tags, not suppressed.
Deleted or superseded facts must be marked as such in relationships (e.g., `predicate: 'obsoleted_by'`) and never presented as current.

details: Policy aligned with atomic decomposition and durability constraints.
Ambiguity preserved via metadata and explicit relationships.
No superseded facts will be exposed as current.

repair_options: Add a `valid_until` timestamp to each memory card for automatic supersession tracking.
Implement a conflict resolution layer to merge duplicate (name, value) pairs under a canonical entity.
Use probabilistic confidence scores for ambiguous (name, value) pairs.
